title: phlomis cypria subsp. occidentalis
intro: Phlomis cypria subsp. occidentalis, commonly known as the Cyprus phlomis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Phlomis cypria subsp. occidentalis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
